hero.subheadline: Clean UI, fast pages, and code your team can actually maintain.

deliveryLog.message: Goals, users, and conversion blockers mapped
Figma or XD translated into pixel-precise React components
Next.js routing, data fetching, and API layer wired
WordPress CMS connected via REST API or headless setup
Legacy dependencies detected — performance pass queued
Accessibility, mobile QA, and Core Web Vitals signed off
Handoff complete — your team owns the codebase

painPoints: Designs stuck in Figma, never shipped
UI that's slow and hard to maintain
problemsSolved: Figma designs that never make it to production cleanly
React codebases no one wants to touch after launch
Slow pages that fail Core Web Vitals
UI that breaks on mobile or at edge-case screen sizes
No component system — every page built from scratch
A WordPress site the team can't update without breaking things
API integrations that were bolted on as an afterthought
Missed deadlines and developers who stop responding
Interfaces that look fine but don't convert visitors
Competitors with a product that just feels more polished
whyWork: Code the next dev can read
Problems flagged early, not at launch
You own it when we're done
outcomes: Editable CMS pages your team can manage
Reusable components instead of one-off sections
Faster production with proven builder workflows
Code structured for the next developer

services.name: React & Next.js development
services.description: Turn a design or rough product idea into a fast, production-ready React or Next.js interface.
services.whatYouGet: Responsive pages, reusable components, routing, integrations, and launch-ready front-end structure.
services.bestFit: Best for SaaS pages, dashboards, product sites, and custom front-end builds.
features: Figma or XD to React — no shortcuts
Next.js routing, SSR, and server components

services.name: WordPress & CMS builds
services.description: Build a website your team can manage without asking a developer for every content change.
services.whatYouGet: Editable templates, CMS fields, page builder setup, content structure, and handoff guidance.
services.bestFit: Best for marketing sites, service businesses, landing pages, and content-heavy websites.
features: Custom themes and page builder builds
ACF, CPT, and editable content templates
REST API and headless WordPress setups

services.name: E-commerce & conversion
services.description: Improve the parts of your store that affect trust, product clarity, and checkout completion.
services.whatYouGet: WooCommerce setup, product UX fixes, checkout improvements, payments, and mobile optimization.
services.bestFit: Best for stores with traffic but weak conversion, messy checkout, or hard-to-manage product pages.
features: Product, cart, and checkout UX fixes

services.name: Figma to responsive websites
services.description: Translate approved Figma layouts into responsive pages that match the design and hold up in production.
services.whatYouGet: Pixel-aware section builds, responsive states, reusable UI parts, image optimization, and QA against the source design.
services.bestFit: Best for founders, designers, and agencies with finished Figma files that need clean implementation.
features: Figma frames converted into responsive pages
Desktop, tablet, and mobile layout checks
Font, spacing, and visual state matching
Review pass against the original design

services.name: HTML, CSS & JavaScript development
services.description: Build fast static websites, landing pages, and front-end interactions without adding a heavy framework when the project does not need one.
services.whatYouGet: Semantic HTML, responsive CSS, clean JavaScript interactions, forms, animations, and deployment-ready files.
services.bestFit: Best for brochure sites, campaign pages, lightweight prototypes, and custom front-end sections.
features: Forms, menus, sliders, and UI states

services.name: Website redesign & UI cleanup
services.description: Redesign outdated pages, clean up weak hierarchy, and make the site feel more credible without losing the existing business context.
services.whatYouGet: Homepage or page redesign, layout cleanup, stronger CTAs, mobile fixes, typography pass, and implementation guidance.
services.bestFit: Best for live websites that look dated, feel unclear, or fail to turn visitors into inquiries.
features: Visual hierarchy and section flow improvements
CTA, copy, and conversion path cleanup
Typography, color, and UI polish pass

services.name: JavaScript fixes & integrations
services.description: Fix broken front-end behavior, wire forms and APIs, and improve existing JavaScript without turning a small issue into a full rebuild.
services.whatYouGet: Bug fixes, UI behavior cleanup, form handling, API connections, tracking scripts, and browser compatibility checks.
services.bestFit: Best for sites with broken menus, forms, sliders, scripts, tracking, or third-party integration problems.

process.title: Discovery
process.description: Goals, users, and blockers mapped before a line of code is written.

process.title: Planning
process.description: Component structure, milestones, and review checkpoints set upfront — no surprises mid-build.

process.title: Development
process.description: Built in tight iterations. You see real progress — not a big reveal at the end.

process.title: Launch & Handoff
process.description: QA, deployment, and clean handoff. Your team gets full access and owns it from day one.

about.story: I'm a Front-End Engineer based in Faisalabad, Pakistan. I build React and Next.js interfaces for startups, agencies, and product teams — and WordPress sites for businesses that need a CMS their team can actually manage.
about.motivation: Most of the projects I'm brought onto have the same root problem: a UI that was built fast but not built to last. Hard to extend, slow to load, impossible to hand off. I fix that — and make sure the next developer doesn't have to start from scratch.
about.education: Master of Computer Science, University of Sahiwal

experienceLog.title: Frontend Website Developer
experienceLog.description: Building custom WordPress and React-based websites, developing reusable components, and creating internal tools such as Brickify to improve development workflows.

experienceLog.title: Senior Website Developer
experienceLog.description: Delivered WordPress, React, and Next.js projects, transformed Figma designs into production-ready websites, and developed scalable CMS solutions.

experienceLog.description: Developed responsive websites and web applications using JavaScript, jQuery, AJAX, Angular, and modern front-end practices.

experienceLog.title: Frontend Developer Intern
experienceLog.description: Started my professional development journey by building responsive interfaces and learning production-level development workflows.

faqs.question: Do you work on React and Next.js projects only, or WordPress too?
faqs.answer: Both — React and Next.js are my primary stack. I also build and maintain WordPress sites, especially when a client needs a CMS their non-technical team can manage day to day.

faqs.question: How much does a project cost?
faqs.answer: It depends on scope, complexity, and integrations. I give you a clear quote once we've aligned on what the project actually needs — no guesswork, no scope creep surprises.

faqs.question: How long does a typical project take?
faqs.answer: Most React or Next.js projects take 2–4 weeks after designs are ready. WordPress sites typically run 1–3 weeks. Either way, you get a timeline upfront and regular progress you can see.

faqs.question: Can you take over an existing codebase or site?
faqs.answer: Yes — that's a common ask. I can refactor components, improve performance, add features to an existing React app, or clean up a WordPress site so it's faster and easier to manage.
